item.title: Wars trigger $12bn venture capital rush into defence tech
item.description: Funding this year has already surpassed 2025 total as soaring valuations fuel fears of a hype cycle

item.title: Michael O’Leary in line for €150mn payout in latest Ryanair contract
item.description: Longtime boss of Europe’s biggest airline signs new deal that includes bumper share award if he hits ambitious targets

item.title: Electricity ‘reselling’ adds £99mn to consumer bills
item.description: Regulator blames market design as hydropower stations and batteries repeatedly sell and curtail their output

item.title: Michael Bloomberg arms green lobby for fight against oil interests
item.description: Billionaire pledges $285mn to renewable industry associations as nations face energy policy decisions in wake of Iran war

item.title: UK telecoms groups introduce ‘kill switch’ to cut phone theft from stores
item.description: Virgin Media O2 and VodafoneThree’s move comes after Apple and Samsung resisted calls to adopt broader measures

item.title: SpaceX handed lowest possible ESG rating by MSCI
item.description: Triple C score puts Elon Musk’s company on par with Russia after 2022 invasion of Ukraine

item.title: Workers are emerging as the next big AI logjam
item.description: Big Tech wakes up to the need for brawn and crafts skills to build and maintain data centres

item.title: Holiday bookings surge in markets hit by Iran crisis
item.description: Rebound across eastern Mediterranean as travellers reassess conflict’s threat and proximity

item.title: Sorry, Elon, the far right is still the far right
item.description: And on some issues, it’s further to the right than before

item.title: Putin’s war machine sputters in drone age
item.description: Ukraine’s innovations cut into Russia’s manpower advantage

item.title: ‘You’re dead’ — Europe’s SpaceX fights back against Musk prophecy
item.description: Ariane 6 rocket programme gets help from Jeff Bezos as it seeks to emerge from US rival’s shadow

item.title: Insurers reach for Latin dictionary to rebrand practice that angered Trump
item.description: Notices of cancellation sent out by marine insurers after war broke out in the Gulf could be renamed ‘denunciato recisiones’

item.title: Cancer drugmaker Revolution Medicines says sale not a priority
item.description: Sought-after biotech company pushes back against being scooped up following Merck’s unsuccessful $32bn bid

item.title: Renault to abstain on Nissan board appointment
item.description: Non-executive director was proponent of push to merge Japanese group with rival Honda

item.title: Did Anthropic talk its way into an AI export ban?
item.description: FT analysis shows company warned about dangers of advanced AI far more than rival OpenAI this year

item.title: Will football in America stick?
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Kansas City Chiefs president discusses hosting the World Cup

item.title: Ministers to make YouTube and Meta boost prominence of UK news
item.description: Move expected in British government green paper would set stage for fresh battle with Big Tech over online misinformation

item.title: Rathbones’ blunder boosts banks in the race for the UK’s rich
item.description: An internal review that found regulatory compliance shortcomings sent the stock down 17%

item.title: Using AI for financial advice? Proceed with caution
item.description: The chatbots are helpful for simple tasks, but they can make costly mistakes

item.title: Big Tech is stoking unrest in the UK. Why?
item.description: Elon Musk’s amplification of anti-immigrant sentiment in Belfast, Southampton and beyond cannot be explained by ideology alone

item.title: ChatGPT moved my cheese: AI is unsettling the self-help shelf
item.description: Instant summaries sound the death knell for the bullet-point books that prey on our insecurities

item.title: Take our phones too! Let’s add adults to the social media ban
item.description: We have to face up to the fact that we’re all addicted to online scrolling

item.title: Fertiliser prices tumble as traders look beyond Middle East disruption
item.description: Urea back to prewar levels but drop in demand ‘is not good news’

item.title: Bob Iger’s long goodbye
item.description: For two decades, Hollywood’s most indispensable executive has been synonymous with Disney. Can he really tear himself away?

item.title: Why are we not talking to one another?
item.description: Conversations with a chatbot will never provide the same human nourishment

item.title: Beer can boost ‘spiritual or mental’ health, says Kirin president
item.description: Japanese brewer’s Takeshi Minakata says alcohol ‘can be a good thing’ as it ‘helps make people happy’

item.title: America’s intractable air traffic problem
item.description: Tech outages and staff shortages are causing traveller misery as World Cup fans arrive and summer travel peaks

item.title: UK unveils prototype missiles for Ukraine with no US components
item.description: Low-cost, long-range weapons could be deployed as soon as this year in war against Russia

item.title: One person dead and 89 injured after UK train crash
item.description: Collision on the Midland main line is the first fatal rail incident in nearly two years

item.title: Trump accepts Qatar jet as new Air Force One despite ethics concerns
item.description: The $400mn gift has raised charges of conflicts between personal interests and official duties
